title: Auxiliary next-compound choice model feeding the pit classifier
summary: Public compound-decision work treats tyre choice after a stop as its own prediction problem.
rationale: Public compound-decision work treats tyre choice after a stop as its own prediction problem. For next-lap pit prediction, knowing whether the current state strongly points toward a plausible incoming compound can add strategy context that a pure binary label misses.
implementation_hint: Within each driver-race sequence, derive `next_compound_after_pit` from actual pit events. Train a multiclass OOF model on pit rows only, using current race state to predict the next compound class. Feed the resulting class probabilities, entropy, and top-margin back into the main `PitNextLap` model for all rows using neutral defaults where needed.
expected_effect: Adds branch-selection information around one-stop vs two-stop decisions and may sharpen probabilities near genuine pit windows.
risk: Label extraction around pit-in/pit-out rows or wet races can be noisy, and this must be done strictly OOF to avoid future-label leakage.
